$meta.reason_for_update: v3.0 used binary-repagging which only renamed Chromium. v4.0 implements a true source build pipeline identical to how Helium, Bromite, and Cromite build real browsers with real features.
$meta.generated_for: GitHub Copilot Agent (VS Code MCP Mode)
$meta.author: Shoislam0311

$instructions_for_agent.behavior: You are a senior Chromium browser engineer. This JSON is your ONLY source of truth. Execute every phase exactly as written. Before writing any C++ or GN code, use Context7 MCP to verify API names and file paths against the current Chromium source tree. Use Sequential Thinking MCP to plan each phase before executing. Use Filesystem MCP to read/write all local files. Use GitHub MCP for all remote repository operations. Use Playwright MCP for all browser UI tests. Reference the three implementations listed in $meta.reference_implementations as your primary learning source.
non_negotiable_rules: NEVER use binary repagging — this is a full source build
NEVER use depot_tools — ungoogled-chromium has its own fetch tooling
NEVER install or reference uBlock Origin as an extension — MV3 broke it on Chromium; use subresource_filter component instead
NEVER use GitHub-hosted runners for compilation — they lack disk and RAM; self-hosted only
NEVER add telemetry, analytics, crash reporting, or any Google API keys
NEVER commit secrets, tokens, or credentials
NEVER hardcode absolute paths — use environment variables and $(pwd) references
NEVER skip a verification_gate — each gate must pass before the next phase begins
ALWAYS port privacy patches from Cromite/Bromite rather than writing from scratch
ALWAYS pin Chromium version in chromium_version.txt for reproducibility
ALWAYS use clang, never GCC, for Chromium compilation
ALWAYS use set -euo pipefail in every shell script

code_style.shell: bash strict mode: set -euo pipefail, all functions documented
code_style.python: PEP8, type-annotated, Python 3.10+, no external deps beyond stdlib
code_style.gn: GN build language, verified against current Chromium gn reference
code_style.json: 2-space indent, no trailing commas, no comments

project.name: Nickel Browser
project.tagline: The Privacy-First Browser That Puts Community Over Corporation
project.slogan: Block Everything. Leak Nothing. Own Your Web.

system_requirements.critical_note: These are HARD REQUIREMENTS. Building Chromium from source is resource-intensive. Attempting a source build without meeting these requirements will result in failed builds or out-of-memory errors.

minimum.os: Ubuntu 22.04 LTS or Debian 12 (Linux is primary; macOS/Windows build support is secondary)

recommended.note: With 16 cores, a full build takes approximately 2-3 hours. With 8 cores, expect 4-8 hours.

system_requirements.ci_runner_requirement: MUST use self-hosted runners. GitHub-hosted runners have max 14GB disk and 7GB RAM — completely insufficient for Chromium source builds.

build_strategy.why_not_binary_repagging: Binary repagging only renames/rebrands a pre-built binary. It CANNOT add C++ features, privacy patches, fingerprint protection, source-level ad blocking, WebRTC leak protection, or any of Nickel's core features. Everything in this specification requires source code modification and recompilation.

pipeline_overview.step_1: Fetch Chromium source via ungoogled-chromium's downloads.py (~35GB, one-time)
pipeline_overview.step_2: Apply ungoogled-chromium patch series (removes all Google infrastructure)
pipeline_overview.step_3: Run ungoogled-chromium binary pruning (removes proprietary blobs)
pipeline_overview.step_4: Run ungoogled-chromium domain substitution (kills Google domain hardcoding)
pipeline_overview.step_5: Apply Nickel's own patch series (privacy + features + branding + adblocking)
pipeline_overview.step_8: ninja compilation (~4-8 hours full, ~10-30 min incremental)

reference_build_commands.note: These are the actual commands scripts/build-nickel.sh must orchestrate

repository_structure.description: Generate this EXACT directory and file tree. Every file listed must be created. Do not create dist/ or chromium-src/ (they are gitignored).

root_version_files.chromium_version.txt: Contains ONLY the pinned Chromium version string, e.g. 136.0.7103.92. Updated manually when upgrading Chromium base.
root_version_files.revision.txt: Contains the ungoogled-chromium git revision hash corresponding to the Chromium version. Format: <uc_version>-1 e.g. 136.0.7103.92-1
root_version_files.downloads.ini: INI-format file listing Chromium source archives and their SHA-512 checksums. Format inherited from ungoogled-chromium's downloads.ini schema.
root_version_files.deps.ini: Third-party dependency versions and download URLs for build tools not in the Chromium tree.
root_version_files.pruning.list: Newline-separated list of binary files to remove from Chromium source (inherited from ungoogled-chromium, extended by Nickel)
root_version_files.domain_substitution.list: Regex-based domain substitution rules (inherited from ungoogled-chromium). Replaces google.com references in source.

utils.description: Python build utility scripts — core of the source build pipeline. Modeled after ungoogled-chromium's utils/ directory.
utils.downloads.py: Fetches Chromium source archives defined in downloads.ini. Verifies SHA-512. Extracts to target directory. Handles resume on partial download.
utils.patches.py: Applies a quilt-style patch series to a target directory. Supports: apply, reverse, check (dry-run). Takes series file path and target directory as arguments.
utils.prune_binaries.py: Removes files listed in pruning.list from the Chromium source tree. These are proprietary Google binaries replaced with open-source alternatives.
utils.domain_sub.py: Applies regex domain substitutions from domain_substitution.list to all text files in the Chromium source. Replaces hardcoded Google domain references.
utils.replace_resources.py: Swaps Chromium branding assets (logos, icons, strings) with Nickel equivalents. Maps source paths in src/nickel/branding/ to target paths in Chromium source.
utils.generate_ruleset.py: Converts EasyList/EasyPrivacy .txt filter lists into Chromium's subresource_filter flatbuffer ruleset binary format. Required for source-level ad blocking.
utils.update_patches.py: Helper to refresh patches after a Chromium version bump. Runs git am on each patch and reports failures.
utils.check_patches.py: Dry-run patch validation — checks all patches in patches/nickel/series apply cleanly without actually modifying the tree.
utils.build_info.py: Generates build_info.json with: nickel_version, chromium_version, uc_revision, build_date_utc, build_host_os, git_commit_hash.

patches.description: ALL patch files in unified diff format (-u). Applied in order defined in their respective series files.

ungoogled_chromium.description: DO NOT WRITE THESE. Fetch them directly from the ungoogled-chromium repository at the revision pinned in revision.txt. Store as a git submodule or copy into this directory.
ungoogled_chromium.series: Inherited from ungoogled-chromium — do not modify order

nickel.series: Ordered list of all Nickel patches. MUST list every patch filename in exact application order.

0001-product-name-nickel.patch.description: Replace all occurrences of 'Chromium' product name with 'Nickel Browser' in chrome/app/chromium_strings.grd and all .grd/.grdp files. Replace 'Google Chrome' with 'Nickel Browser'.
0001-product-name-nickel.patch.method: sed-based string substitution via replace_resources.py, not a traditional code patch

0002-remove-google-branding.patch.description: Remove Google logo assets, Google icon references, and Google-specific UI elements from chrome/app/theme/ and related files.

0003-about-version-branding.patch.description: Patch chrome/browser/ui/webui/version_ui.cc to show Nickel Browser version info, acknowledgments, and remove Chromium/Google references from about:version page.

0004-user-agent-string.patch.description: Set user agent to: Mozilla/5.0 ... Chrome/<chromium_version> NickelBrowser/<nickel_version>. Do NOT expose as Chromium or Chrome — use neutral UA that passes web compatibility while hiding fingerprint.

0005-custom-ntp-registration.patch.description: Register Nickel's custom New Tab Page as the browser's NTP. Adds WebUI handler for chrome://newtab routing to nickel_ntp_ui.cc.

0010-disable-webrtc-ip-leak.patch.description: Restrict WebRTC ICE candidate gathering to prevent real IP leakage when behind VPN/proxy. Force mDNS obfuscation for host candidates. Block reflexive candidates that expose public IP.
0010-disable-webrtc-ip-leak.patch.source_reference: Port from ungoogled-chromium: 0001-webrtc-ice-candidate-restraints.patch — already exists, verify it is in UC patches before duplicating
0010-disable-webrtc-ip-leak.patch.settings_integration: Add toggle to chrome://settings/privacy: 'WebRTC IP Handling Policy' with options: default, disable_non_proxied_udp, proxy_only

0011-canvas-fingerprint-noise.patch.description: Add per-session deterministic noise to canvas pixel readback APIs. Noise is seeded with a per-session random key (NOT per-call — breaks legitimate canvas use if per-call). getImageData(), toDataURL(), toBlob() all return slightly modified pixel values imperceptible to humans but breaking cross-session fingerprinting.
0011-canvas-fingerprint-noise.patch.source_reference: Port from Cromite: patches/cromite/canvas-image-data-noise.patch — study their exact implementation before writing
0011-canvas-fingerprint-noise.patch.implementation_note: Use RendererPreferences to pass session noise seed from browser process to renderer. Noise magnitude: ±1 per RGB channel maximum.

0012-webgl-vendor-spoof.patch.description: Return generic GPU vendor/renderer strings from WebGL UNMASKED_VENDOR_WEBGL and UNMASKED_RENDERER_WEBGL extensions. Return 'Mesa' and 'Mesa Intel' regardless of actual hardware.
0012-webgl-vendor-spoof.patch.additional_hardening: Consider completely removing WEBGL_debug_renderer_info extension as a Nickel-specific enhancement — no legitimate web app needs actual GPU vendor

0013-audiocontext-fingerprint-noise.patc.description: Add imperceptible noise to AudioContext getFloatFrequencyData() and getByteFrequencyData() outputs. Use same session noise seed as canvas patch for correlation resistance.

0014-hardware-api-restrictions.patch.description: Restrict hardware information APIs that are used for fingerprinting. Implementation: navigator.getBattery() returns a Promise that resolves to null. navigator.deviceMemory always returns 8 (fixed). navigator.hardwareConcurrency always returns 8 (fixed). navigator.connection returns undefined.

0015-font-enumeration-block.patch.description: Block the Local Font Access API (navigator.fonts) which exposes installed fonts for fingerprinting. Return empty iterator or throw NotAllowedError.

0016-first-party-isolation.patch.description: Enable storage partitioning (First-Party Isolation / Total Cookie Protection equivalent). Partition cookies, localStorage, IndexedDB, Cache API, and service workers by top-level eTLD+1. This prevents cross-site tracking via shared storage.
0016-first-party-isolation.patch.note: Chromium has partial support for storage partitioning built-in. Check what Privacy Sandbox has already implemented before patching to avoid duplication.

0017-https-only-mode-default.patch.description: Set HTTPS-Only Mode to ENABLED by default (not just available). When HTTP URL is requested, attempt HTTPS upgrade first. Show interstitial if HTTPS unavailable (do NOT silently fall back to HTTP).
0017-https-only-mode-default.patch.implementation: Change default pref value of prefs::kHttpsOnlyModeEnabled from false to true in browser_prefs.cc

0018-doh-quad9-default.patch.description: Set DNS-over-HTTPS provider to Quad9 (https://dns.quad9.net/dns-query) by default. Enable DoH in Secure mode (not Automatic) so all DNS goes encrypted. Remove Google DNS and Cloudflare from default provider list or clearly label them.

0019-block-google-internal-domains.patch.description: Block connections to Google internal telemetry domains at the network layer. These are NOT domains users navigate to — they are background telemetry pings that survive even ungoogled-chromium patches in edge cases.
0019-block-google-internal-domains.patch.note: Most of these are already handled by ungoogled-chromium's domain substitution. Apply this patch AFTER UC patches and check for duplicates.

0020-screen-resolution-normalize.patch.description: Normalize screen resolution and color depth values reported to JavaScript. Report standard resolutions instead of actual hardware values to prevent screen-based fingerprinting.
0020-screen-resolution-normalize.patch.implementation: Round screen.width and screen.height to nearest 100px. Fix screen.colorDepth at 24.

0021-timezone-protection.patch.description: Protect timezone from being used as fingerprint vector. Allow user to optionally set a fixed timezone override. Default: no change (timezone is not spoofed by default as it breaks too many legitimate use cases, but provide the toggle).
0021-timezone-protection.patch.implementation: Add chrome://settings/privacy toggle: 'Override timezone to UTC for privacy'

adblocking.critical_architecture_note: DO NOT use uBlock Origin as an extension. MV3 in Chromium has severely limited its effectiveness. Instead, use Chromium's BUILT-IN subresource_filter component which operates at the C++ network layer — completely invisible to JavaScript, undetectable by anti-adblock scripts, and cannot be disabled by web pages.
adblocking.reference: See chromium-src/components/subresource_filter/FILTER_LIST_GENERATION.md for the official filter list format documentation.
adblocking.how_subresource_filter_works: Chromium's subresource_filter is the same engine Google uses for Safe Browsing ad filtering. It intercepts URLRequests before they are dispatched at the network service layer. Filter rules are compiled into a flatbuffer binary format and loaded at browser startup. This is what Bromite and Cromite both use.

0030-enable-subresource-filter-always.pa.description: Enable Chromium's subresource_filter component unconditionally (not just when Safe Browsing is active). Normally it only activates for Safe Browsing-flagged pages. Patch removes this restriction so filtering applies globally.
0030-enable-subresource-filter-always.pa.source_reference: Port from Bromite: patches/Add-new-permission-for-NFC.patch (find their subresource filter always-on patch)
0030-enable-subresource-filter-always.pa.implementation: Remove the Safe Browsing activation requirement. Set activation_state to ENABLED for all pages unconditionally.

0031-load-nickel-filter-ruleset.patch.description: Load Nickel's compiled filter ruleset (nickel-filters.fb) at browser startup. The ruleset is embedded in the browser's resource pak file by inject-filter-lists.sh during build.
0031-load-nickel-filter-ruleset.patch.implementation: Add a new RulesetVersion entry for Nickel filters. Load from embedded resources first, then allow user profile directory override for runtime filter updates.

0032-cosmetic-filter-engine.patch.description: Implement CSS cosmetic filtering (element hiding) to remove ad containers that survive network blocking. Inject CSS rules via content script mechanism after document DOM is constructed.
0032-cosmetic-filter-engine.patch.implementation: Add a new content script injection point that loads cosmetic_filters.css from browser resources and injects it into every page's document. CSS rules come from EasyList cosmetic filter entries.

0033-cname-uncloaking.patch.description: Detect and block CNAME-cloaked trackers. Many ad/tracking networks hide behind first-party CNAME entries. When a DNS response returns a CNAME pointing to a known tracker domain, block the request even though the hostname looked first-party.
0033-cname-uncloaking.patch.source_reference: Port from uBlock Origin's CNAME uncloaking approach, adapted for C++ network service layer

0034-anti-adblock-detection-evasion.patc.description: Prevent websites from detecting that ad blocking is active. Technique: return fake ad element placeholders (1x1 transparent images) for blocked ad requests instead of network errors. This makes navigator.onLine and ad detection scripts believe ads loaded successfully.

0035-youtube-ad-blocking.patch.description: Specific patch for YouTube ad blocking beyond what EasyList covers. Block ytInitialPlayerResponse ad insertion points and prebaked ad payloads. Reference Cromite's YouTube-specific patches.
0035-youtube-ad-blocking.patch.source_reference: Port from Cromite: look for YouTube-specific filter/blocking patches in their patch series

0036-sponsorblock-integration.patch.description: Integrate SponsorBlock segment skipping for YouTube. Query the SponsorBlock API (https://sponsor.ajay.app) for the current video ID and skip sponsor/intro/outro segments automatically. Make this opt-in with a chrome://settings toggle since it requires an external API call.
0036-sponsorblock-integration.patch.privacy_note: SponsorBlock API calls expose which YouTube videos are being watched. Must be clearly disclosed and default OFF. User must explicitly enable it.

features.note: Each feature patch must be self-contained. If a feature requires both browser process (C++) and renderer process changes, split into browser-side and renderer-side patches.

0040-vertical-tabs-sidebar.patch.description: Add a vertical tab strip sidebar panel to the browser window. Tabs appear as a collapsible list on the left side. Default ON. Includes: tab thumbnails, tab groups, drag-to-reorder, collapse/expand.
0040-vertical-tabs-sidebar.patch.source_reference: Study Microsoft Edge's vertical tabs implementation approach. Also reference Helium's sidebar implementation.

0041-workspaces.patch.description: Implement named browser workspaces (separate tab sets with independent cookie jars, optionally). User can switch between Work, Personal, Shopping workspaces. Each workspace is a separate BrowserContext.

0042-split-view.patch.description: Enable side-by-side tab viewing. Two tabs displayed simultaneously in a split pane. Splitter is user-draggable. Toggle via toolbar button or keyboard shortcut (Ctrl+Shift+\).

0043-container-tabs.patch.description: Firefox Multi-Account Containers equivalent. Each container has isolated cookies, localStorage, and cache. Containers are color-coded and named by user (Personal, Work, Banking, Shopping). Sites can be auto-assigned to containers.

0044-tab-sleep.patch.description: Auto-suspend tabs that have been inactive for a configurable period (default: 30 minutes). Suspended tabs release renderer memory. Tab title prefixed with [Sleeping]. Reactivated when user clicks the tab.

0045-session-manager.patch.description: Named session save/restore. User can save all open windows and tabs as a named session. Sessions persist across browser restarts. Accessible from menu: Sessions > Save Current / Restore Saved.

0046-ipfs-protocol-handler.patch.description: Add native ipfs:// URL scheme handler. Route IPFS requests through a configurable gateway (default: https://ipfs.io/ipfs/). Allow user to set custom gateway or run local IPFS daemon.

0047-mouse-gestures.patch.description: Right-click hold + directional mouse gesture navigation. Gestures: Right=Forward, Left=Back, Up=New Tab, Down=Close Tab, Up-Right=Scroll Top, Down-Right=Scroll Bottom.

0048-pip-global-button.patch.description: Add a permanent Picture-in-Picture button overlay on any HTML5 video element on hover. Not just YouTube — works on any video. Clicking the button extracts video to floating PiP window.

0049-download-manager-aria2.patch.description: Replace Chromium's single-threaded downloader with aria2c-based multi-threaded download manager. If aria2c is installed, route all downloads through it. Show download progress, speed, and ETA in a proper download manager panel.
0049-download-manager-aria2.patch.fallback: If aria2c not found, fall back to Chromium's built-in downloader with a notification to install aria2c

0050-json-xml-viewer.patch.description: Auto-detect and pretty-print JSON and XML responses in browser tab (not just DevTools). When Content-Type is application/json or application/xml, render as collapsible tree with syntax highlighting instead of raw text.

0051-qr-code-generator.patch.description: Add QR code generation to the right-click context menu ('Generate QR Code for this URL') and to the address bar as an icon button. Use a pure C++ QR library (no external service).

0052-notes-sidebar.patch.description: Per-page sticky notes panel in the side panel. Notes are associated with the page URL and persist in user profile. Accessible via side panel icon or Ctrl+Shift+N.

0053-keyboard-shortcut-remapper.patch.description: Allow users to customize any browser keyboard shortcut from a dedicated page (chrome://settings/keyboard-shortcuts or accessible from Settings > Keyboard). Store custom mappings in user profile.

0054-exif-scrubber.patch.description: Strip EXIF/XMP/IPTC metadata from JPEG, PNG, TIFF, and WEBP images when downloading or saving. Show notification: 'Metadata stripped from [filename]'. Toggle in Settings > Privacy: 'Strip metadata from downloaded images'.

0055-local-password-vault.patch.description: Replace Chromium's Google-synced password manager with a local-only KeePass-compatible encrypted vault. Encryption: AES-256-GCM with PBKDF2 key derivation. Support import from KeePass .kdbx files and from Chromium's existing password export CSV.

0056-tor-private-tab.patch.description: Add Tor-routed private browsing mode. When user opens a Tor Private Tab (Ctrl+Shift+T), all requests for that tab are routed through a bundled Tor client (tor binary shipped with Nickel). Regular private tabs are unchanged. Tor tabs show onion icon in address bar.
0056-tor-private-tab.patch.tor_binary_note: Bundle the official Tor binary from https://www.torproject.org as a resource. License: BSD-3-Clause compatible.

0057-vpn-client-wireguard.patch.description: Built-in WireGuard VPN client. User can configure WireGuard server details from Settings > VPN. When active, all browser traffic routes through the VPN tunnel. Show VPN status in toolbar. Default: disconnected (user must configure).
0057-vpn-client-wireguard.patch.note: WireGuard userspace library (wireguard-go or boringssl-based) must be bundled. This is a COMPLEX feature — mark as v1.1 if not completing in v1.0.

0060-block-doubleclick-domains.patch.description: Add *.doubleclick.net, *.googleadservices.com, and *.googlesyndication.com to a compile-time blocked domains list. Requests to these domains return BLOCKED status before reaching network stack.

0061-referrer-policy-strict.patch.description: Override default Referrer-Policy to 'strict-origin-when-cross-origin' (instead of no-referrer-when-downgrade). For privacy-sensitive contexts, use 'no-referrer'. Add toggle in Settings > Privacy.

branding.product_logo_16.png: 16x16 PNG for Chromium product logo override
branding.resource_map.json: Maps Chromium source asset paths to Nickel replacement paths. Used by utils/replace_resources.py.
branding.strings_override.grd: GRD format overrides for all user-visible strings replacing Chromium/Google branding with Nickel branding

args.gn.description: Complete GN build arguments for Nickel Browser source build. CRITICAL: Every argument name MUST be verified against the current Chromium GN reference using Context7 MCP before writing. Wrong argument names cause silent build failures.

build.BUILD.gn: Nickel-specific GN build targets: nickel_ntp, nickel_settings, nickel_password_vault, nickel_tor_integration

filter_lists.note: These are the raw .txt filter lists committed to repo. utils/generate_ruleset.py compiles them into the binary flatbuffer format during build.
filter_lists.ublock-filters.txt: uBlock Origin built-in filter list (the FILTERS not the extension)
filter_lists.annoyances.txt: Cookie banners, newsletter popups, GDPR notices
filter_lists.nickel-custom.txt: Nickel's own curated filter rules — community-maintained
filter_lists.cosmetic-filters.css: Compiled CSS for element hiding rules (generated from filter lists' cosmetic entries)

ntp.description: Custom New Tab Page — delivered as a Chromium WebUI page (chrome://newtab). Pure HTML/CSS/JS, zero external requests, all assets bundled, <50KB total.
ntp.index.html: NTP entry point — registered as a WebUI resource
ntp.ntp.css: Styles using Nickel brand colors from colors.json. Dark theme default.
ntp.ntp.js: NTP logic: clock (12/24h configurable), search bar (DuckDuckGo default), quick links grid, privacy stats counter (ads blocked, trackers blocked since install)
ntp.icons/: SVG icons for NTP (search, settings, clock) — no external icon font CDN
ntp.nickel_ntp_ui.cc: C++ WebUI controller registering the NTP with Chromium's WebUI factory
ntp.nickel_ntp.pak: Compiled pak resource bundle for NTP assets — generated during build

ui.toolbar_icons/: SVG replacements for Chromium's default toolbar icons

scripts.common.sh: Shared library: log functions, require_command(), detect_platform(), get_chromium_version(), get_nickel_version(), make_temp_dir(), check_disk_space(required_gb), check_ram(required_gb)

setup-build-env.sh.description: One-time setup script: installs all build dependencies on the host system

fetch-chromium-source.sh.description: Downloads Chromium source code. Uses utils/downloads.py. This is a ~35GB download. Script is resumable.
key_logic: Print exact UC revision being used
Warn if disk space < 250GB before starting

prepare-source.sh.description: Applies all patches and transformations to the Chromium source tree. Must be run after fetch-chromium-source.sh.
stages: Stage 1: Apply ungoogled-chromium patch series (python3 utils/patches.py apply chromium-src/ patches/ungoogled-chromium/)
Stage 2: Prune proprietary binaries (python3 utils/prune_binaries.py chromium-src/ pruning.list)
Stage 3: Domain substitution (python3 utils/domain_sub.py apply chromium-src/ domain_substitution.list)
Stage 4: Apply Nickel patch series (python3 utils/patches.py apply chromium-src/ patches/nickel/)
Stage 5: Replace branding resources (python3 utils/replace_resources.py chromium-src/ src/nickel/branding/)
Stage 6: Compile filter lists to ruleset (python3 utils/generate_ruleset.py src/nickel/filter_lists/ chromium-src/components/subresource_filter/core/common/rulesets/)

configure-build.sh.description: Runs GN configuration to generate build system files
logic: Detect platform and set target_os and target_cpu in args
Log: 'Build configured. Run scripts/compile.sh to start compilation.'

compile.sh.description: Runs ninja compilation. This is the long step (4-8 hours first run).
logic: On completion: print binary size and location
On failure: print last 100 lines of build log and exit 1
Incremental builds (after first run) take 10-30 minutes

time_expectations.note: Warn user before starting: 'Full build will take 4-8 hours. Keep terminal open.'

build-nickel.sh.description: Master build orchestrator — runs all steps in order
steps: Check system requirements (disk, RAM, CPU, tools)
fetch-chromium-source.sh (skip if chromium-src/ exists and version matches)

scripts.build-deb.sh: Creates .deb package from compiled output in chromium-src/out/Nickel/
scripts.generate-checksums.sh: SHA-256 checksums for all dist/ artifacts
scripts.update-filter-lists.sh: Fetches fresh filter lists from upstream sources — run by CI nightly
scripts.check-patches.sh: Dry-run validation of all patches in patches/nickel/ series

github_workflows.self_hosted_runner_note: ALL compilation jobs MUST run on self-hosted runners. GitHub-hosted runners have max 14GB disk and 7GB RAM. Chromium source build requires 250GB disk and 16GB RAM minimum. Set up a self-hosted runner on a machine meeting system_requirements.minimum specs.

steps: Cache chromium-src/ by chromium_version.txt hash (saves re-downloading 35GB)
same as linux but calls build-dmg.sh
same as linux but calls build-windows-installer.sh
Upload to GitHub Release using softprops/action-gh-release@v2

nickel-pr-check.yml.note: PR checks do NOT compile — they only validate code quality and patch syntax
steps: Check chromium_version.txt and revision.txt are in sync
Verify filter list files are non-empty and have valid headers
Check no secrets with trufflehog or gitleaks
Upload SARIF to GitHub Security tab
Check filter lists staleness (warn if >7 days old)
Dependency review for any new tool additions
If lists changed: commit with message 'chore(filters): nightly filter list update YYYY-MM-DD'
Create PR with filter update stats (rules added/removed per list)

triggers: push to main when chromium_version.txt changes
nickel-patch-compat-check.yml.purpose: When Chromium version is bumped, automatically test if all patches still apply cleanly
steps: Attempt to apply each Nickel patch
Report which patches failed and need refresh
Create issue listing patches needing update

required_sections: Quick Start (5 commands from zero to running browser)
Detailed Platform Guides: Linux, macOS, Windows
First Build vs Incremental Builds (time expectations)
Upstream Sources: Cromite, Bromite, Helium, ungoogled-chromium
Source Build Pipeline (step-by-step with file paths)
Privacy Layer Stack (which layer handles which attack vector)

docs.SECURITY.md: Full security policy: threat model, vulnerability disclosure, 90-day deadline, contact info
docs.PRIVACY_POLICY.md: Privacy policy with Wireshark verification guide and network audit table
docs.CONTRIBUTING.md: Full contributor guide: setup, code style, commit format, patch submission, review process, DCO

SELF_HOSTED_RUNNER_SETUP.md.description: Step-by-step guide for setting up a self-hosted GitHub Actions runner that meets build requirements
required_sections: Disk Space Management (build caches can fill 250GB fast)

smoke.launch_test.sh: Launch chromium-src/out/Nickel/chrome, wait 5s, verify process running, verify window title is 'Nickel Browser'
smoke.ntp_test.sh: Open new tab, Playwright screenshot, verify: Nickel logo visible, search bar visible, privacy counter visible, clock visible
smoke.branding_test.sh: Navigate to chrome://version, assert contains 'Nickel Browser', assert does NOT contain 'Google', assert does NOT contain 'Chromium' in product name fields

privacy.telemetry_audit.sh: tcpdump capture during 60s browser idle session. Parse pcap. Assert ZERO connections to: *.google.com, *.gstatic.com, *.googleapis.com, *.chromium.org, *.doubleclick.net, *.googlesyndication.com
privacy.webrtc_leak_test.sh: Playwright: load browserleaks.com/webrtc behind proxy, assert no real IP in ICE candidates
privacy.canvas_noise_test.sh: Run canvas fingerprint test twice in same session — assert same result (deterministic). Run in two sessions — assert different results (session-keyed).
privacy.webgl_spoof_test.sh: Navigate to browserleaks.com/webgl, assert UNMASKED_VENDOR is generic string, not actual GPU vendor
privacy.dns_leak_test.sh: tcpdump on UDP port 53 during browsing session. Assert ZERO plaintext DNS queries (all DNS must go through DoH)

adblocking.easylist_block_test.sh: Load 20 known ad-serving test URLs, assert each returns blocked response
adblocking.benchmark_test.sh: Playwright: navigate to d3ward.github.io/toolz/adblock, wait for completion, assert score >= 95%
adblocking.detection_evasion_test.sh: Playwright: navigate to sites known for anti-adblock scripts, assert no anti-adblock interstitials appear
adblocking.youtube_ad_test.sh: Playwright: navigate to YouTube video, assert video preroll ad is blocked, assert video plays without ad interstitial

playwright.browser_launch.spec.ts: Full launch → NTP load → navigate to 3 sites → verify history → verify back/forward
playwright.vertical_tabs.spec.ts: Open 5 tabs → assert all in sidebar → drag reorder → assert new order → collapse sidebar → assert hidden → expand → assert visible
playwright.workspaces.spec.ts: Create Work workspace → navigate to example.com → switch to Personal workspace → assert different tab set → switch back → assert Work tabs still there
playwright.adblock.spec.ts: Navigate to adblock benchmark → assert >=95% score → navigate to YouTube → assert no ad plays
playwright.privacy_defaults.spec.ts: Open chrome://settings → navigate to Privacy → assert HTTPS-Only mode ON → assert DoH ON pointing to Quad9 → assert WebRTC restriction ON
playwright.container_tabs.spec.ts: Create Work container → open tab in Work container → set cookie → open same URL in default tab → assert cookie NOT shared
playwright.ntp_no_external.spec.ts: Open new tab → intercept all network requests → assert ZERO requests to external domains

phase_0.name: Study Reference Implementations
phase_0.description: Before writing any code, study how real Chromium-based browsers implement what Nickel needs. This phase prevents reinventing the wheel and ensures patches are grounded in proven approaches.

focus: Read utils/downloads.py — understand how source is fetched
Read utils/patches.py — understand how patches are applied
Read buildkit/ or build.py — understand build orchestration
Note exact Chromium version they are currently tracking
tasks.output: Document in docs/ARCHITECTURE.md: 'UC Build Pipeline' section

focus: Read patches/helium/ series — what features they patch
Read their CI workflows — how they handle source builds in CI
Identify any patches relevant to Nickel features
Find canvas noise patch — port to Nickel
Find WebGL spoof patch — port to Nickel
Find AudioContext noise patch — port to Nickel
Read components/subresource_filter/ patches — ad blocking approach

tasks.note: This file exists inside the Chromium source tree. Must fetch Chromium source first or find it on Chromium Code Search (cs.chromium.org)
tasks.purpose: Understand the exact flatbuffer format required for ad blocking filter lists

tasks: Determine latest stable ungoogled-chromium release version
Write corresponding UC revision to revision.txt
Update downloads.ini with correct source URLs and SHA-512 checksums for that version

phase_0.verification_gate: chromium_version.txt, revision.txt, and downloads.ini all exist and are internally consistent. Running python3 utils/downloads.py fetch --dry-run downloads.ini must succeed.

phase_1.name: Repository Initialization
phase_1.description: Create the complete repository structure and GitHub configuration.

tasks.description: Create every directory and file listed in repository_structure. Use .gitkeep for empty directories.

settings.description: 🪙 Nickel Browser — Privacy-First Browser. Block Everything. Leak Nothing. Own Your Web. Built on ungoogled-chromium.

phase_1.verification_gate: git ls-files | wc -l >= 50. All directories in repository_structure exist. GitHub repo topics, labels, and branch protection are configured.

phase_2.name: Python Build Utilities
phase_2.description: Write all Python utilities in utils/. These are the core of the build pipeline.
phase_2.sequential_thinking_prompt: Design a robust Python build utility system for a Chromium fork. The system must: handle 35GB source downloads with resume support, apply hundreds of patches with clear error reporting, perform text substitution across thousands of files, replace binary assets, and convert text filter lists to binary flatbuffer format. Each utility must be independently testable.

requirements: All utils must have main() entry point and argparse CLI
All utils must have comprehensive docstrings
downloads.py must support resumable downloads and SHA-512 verification
patches.py must report patch failures with context (which file, which line)
generate_ruleset.py must produce valid subresource_filter flatbuffer format — verify format against chromium-src/components/subresource_filter/FILTER_LIST_GENERATION.md

phase_2.verification_gate: python3 -m py_compile utils/*.py must pass for all files. python3 utils/check_patches.py --help must show usage. python3 utils/downloads.py --dry-run downloads.ini must succeed.

phase_3.name: Shell Build Scripts
phase_3.description: Write all shell scripts in scripts/ orchestrating the build pipeline.

requirements: Source common.sh at top of every script
All scripts must be idempotent (safe to run twice)
compile.sh must print elapsed time on completion
setup-build-env.sh must detect if tools already installed and skip

phase_3.verification_gate: bash -n scripts/*.sh passes for all scripts. shellcheck scripts/*.sh reports zero errors or warnings.

phase_4.name: GN Build Configuration
phase_4.description: Write the GN configuration files for the Nickel Browser source build.
phase_4.critical_instruction: Use Context7 MCP to look up EVERY GN argument name in the current Chromium source before writing args.gn. Query: 'Chromium GN build arguments list'. Wrong argument names fail silently during GN gen and produce a binary with wrong settings.

tasks.args_gn_must_include: All arguments listed in repository_structure.src.nickel.build.args.gn with verified-correct GN argument names

phase_4.verification_gate: If GN is available: gn format --dry-run src/nickel/build/args.gn exits 0. Document any unverifiable args with TODO comments for CI verification.

phase_5.name: Branding Patches and Resources
phase_5.description: Write branding patches and create all brand asset files.

tasks.files: All files in src/nickel/branding/ including resource_map.json and strings_override.grd

requirements: Dark theme default using brand colors
Privacy stats counter (ads blocked, trackers blocked)

phase_5.verification_gate: python3 utils/check_patches.py patches/nickel/branding/ reports all patches apply cleanly. NTP HTML validates with W3C validator. NTP JS has no syntax errors.

phase_6.name: Privacy Patches
phase_6.description: Port and write all privacy enhancement patches.
phase_6.sequential_thinking_prompt: For each privacy patch, determine: (1) which Chromium source files need modification, (2) whether a proven implementation already exists in Cromite/Bromite, (3) what the exact C++ change is, (4) how to test it. Plan all 12 privacy patches before writing any of them.

sources: Fetch Cromite canvas noise patch from their GitHub
Fetch Helium hardware API restriction patch

phase_6.verification_gate: python3 utils/check_patches.py patches/nickel/privacy/ reports all patches apply cleanly against the Chromium source tree at pinned version.

phase_7.name: Ad Blocking Integration
phase_7.description: Implement source-level ad blocking using Chromium's native subresource_filter component.
phase_7.critical_architecture_note: DO NOT implement uBlock Origin as an extension. MV3 broke it. Use Chromium's subresource_filter component. Read chromium-src/components/subresource_filter/README.md before writing any code.

tasks.description: Read the subresource_filter component documentation and source code to understand: activation model, ruleset format, how to add custom rulesets, how cosmetic filtering hooks in.
Run scripts/update-filter-lists.sh to populate all filter list files with current content. Commit the initial copies.
tasks.then: Verify utils/generate_ruleset.py converts them to valid flatbuffer format

phase_7.verification_gate: After build: Playwright test navigates to d3ward.github.io/toolz/adblock and asserts score >= 95%. YouTube ad test passes.

phase_8.name: Feature Patches
phase_8.description: Write feature patches for vertical tabs, workspaces, split view, and all other Nickel features.
phase_8.sequential_thinking_prompt: Each feature patch modifies Chromium's UI layer (views framework). Plan which files each feature touches, whether it conflicts with any privacy or branding patches already applied, and whether it can be implemented fully in v1.0 or should be deferred. For deferred features: create the patch file as a stub with a TODO header and a chrome://flags entry to gate the incomplete feature.

for_each_patch: Write the patch in valid unified diff format
Include patch metadata header: Description, Source, Target files, Test procedure
Add to patches/nickel/series in correct position after adblocking patches
Run python3 utils/check_patches.py on the new patch before moving to next

phase_8.verification_gate: python3 utils/check_patches.py patches/nickel/ reports all patches apply cleanly. patches/nickel/series lists all patch files in correct order.

phase_9.name: Network Patches and Final Patch Series
phase_9.description: Write network-level blocking patches and finalize the complete patch series file.

tasks.requirement: Every file listed in series must exist. Every patch file must be listed in series.

phase_9.verification_gate: python3 utils/check_patches.py patches/nickel/ --verify-series passes with zero errors. Count of entries in series equals count of .patch files.

phase_10.name: CI/CD Workflows

key_requirements: Production build MUST use self-hosted runners
PR checks MUST run on ubuntu-22.04 (no compilation, just validation)
Chromium source cache MUST be implemented (saves 35GB re-download)
Filter update workflow MUST run on schedule
Patch compat check MUST trigger when chromium_version.txt changes

phase_10.verification_gate: yamllint .github/workflows/*.yml passes with zero errors. actionlint .github/workflows/*.yml passes.

phase_11.name: Documentation

files: README.md (full v3.0 readme with corrected build instructions)
tasks.readme_correction: README must update Quick Start to reflect source build reality: 'Full first build: 4-8 hours, 250GB disk. Incremental builds: 10-30 min.'

phase_11.verification_gate: markdownlint --config .markdownlint.json docs/**/*.md README.md CONTRIBUTING.md SECURITY.md passes with zero errors.

phase_12.name: Testing Suite

playwright_setup: Create tests/playwright/playwright.config.ts pointing to built Nickel binary

phase_12.verification_gate: bash -n tests/**/*.sh passes. npx tsc --noEmit in tests/playwright/ passes. All smoke tests pass against built binary.

phase_13.name: First Build Attempt and Release
phase_13.description: Execute the complete build pipeline on a self-hosted runner and produce the first alpha release artifacts.

good_first_issues: Run Speedometer 3.0 benchmark and submit results
Test build on Arch Linux (write PKGBUILD for AUR)
Add Arabic translation to UI strings
Write Flatpak manifest for Flathub submission
Add dark/light mode auto-detection to NTP
Refresh patch for Chromium upgrade (good for learning patch workflow)

phase_13.verification_gate: GitHub Release v1.0.0-alpha exists with all artifacts. sha256sum -c checksums.sha256 passes for all artifacts. Browser launches and NTP loads on all three platforms.

when_chromium_version_bumps: Run python3 utils/update_patches.py to test which patches need refresh
For each failing patch: apply manually, fix conflicts, regenerate patch with git diff
Run full test suite to verify nothing regressed
The nickel-patch-compat-check.yml workflow automates detection of broken patches
patch_maintenance_strategy.patch_conflict_resolution: When a patch conflicts after UC or Chromium upgrade, create a GitHub issue with label 'patch-conflict'. Any contributor can claim it, fix the conflict, and submit a PR.
patch_maintenance_strategy.upstream_sync_schedule: Follow ungoogled-chromium releases — they typically lag Chromium stable by 1-2 weeks. Nickel should aim to release within 2 weeks of a new UC release.

must_always_pass: python3 utils/check_patches.py patches/nickel/ — all patches apply cleanly
Privacy audit: zero unexpected network connections
No secrets detected by trufflehog or gitleaks
Build is reproducible: two builds from same source produce bit-identical binaries (target for v1.0)

embed_in_about_page_and_readme.name: Helium Browser
embed_in_about_page_and_readme.role: Build pipeline inspiration, patch series approach

embed_in_about_page_and_readme.name: uBlock Origin
embed_in_about_page_and_readme.role: Filter list format and curation methodology
